prompt: Write a dry one-paragraph Amazon review of the experience of waking up.
response:
★★☆☆☆  
“Waking Up” arrived un-ordered at 05:47, packaged in blaring birdsong and an urgent bladder. Installation required blinking through adhesive dreams and accepting a 15-minute loading screen called “existence.” Color saturation is overpowered, the audio mix privileges traffic over birds, and the included coffee voucher never materialized. Respiration and heartbeat run in background without exit option, consuming 100 % of morning motivation. Product ceased to remember yesterday’s save points and auto-subscribed me to a lifetime of Mondays. Cannot return to sender; logistics claim the box is “consciousness” and already open. Proceed only if you enjoy mandatory DLC.
